- `apps/api/src/middleware.ts` - `authMiddleware` checks `context.user` and `context.session`, exposes `AppContext { user, session }`
- All analytics handlers pass `context.user.id` as orgId to service functions
- Contract in `packages/api-routes/src/index.ts` - no org params in any route inputs

### CLI
- `apps/cli/src/commands/enable.ts` - Adds session-end hook, no org selection
- `apps/cli/src/commands/hooks/claude/session-end.ts` - Reads stdin `{session_id, transcript_path, cwd}`, uploads with Bearer token
- `apps/cli/src/lib/credentials.ts` - Stores `{token, apiBaseUrl}` in `~/.rudel/credentials.json`
- `apps/cli/src/lib/uploader.ts` - Calls `client.ingestSession(request)` via oRPC
- `apps/cli/src/lib/types.ts` - `IngestRequest` has no `organizationId` field

### Web
- `apps/web/src/components/analytics/Sidebar.tsx` - Nav sidebar with user avatar, no org switcher
- Router in `apps/web/src/App.tsx`
- Analytics pages use `orpc.analytics.*` hooks

## better-auth Organization Plugin (researched)
The `organization` plugin from `better-auth/plugins` provides:
- **Tables**: `organization` (id, name, slug, logo, metadata), `member` (id, organizationId, userId, role), `invitation` (id, organizationId, email, role, status, expiresAt, inviterId)
- **Session extension**: Adds `activeOrganizationId` column to `session` table
- **Roles**: Built-in `owner`, `admin`, `member` roles with configurable access control
- **Server API**: `auth.api.getFullOrganization()`, `auth.api.getActiveMember()`, etc.
- **Client API**: `authClient.organization.create()`, `.setActive()`, `.inviteMember()`, `.listOrganizations()`, etc.
- **React hooks**: `authClient.useActiveOrganization()`, `authClient.useListOrganizations()`
- Invitation flow: create invitation -> send email callback -> user accepts via `acceptInvitation({invitationId})`

## Requirements
1. Every user can be in multiple organizations
2. Every organization can have multiple users
3. Users can be invited via link/invite code, or admin can add by email
4. Organization dropdown in sidebar top-left for switching
5. All analytics are scoped to the active organization
6. CLI `enable` command asks which organization to upload sessions for (per-repository config)
7. Session-end hook sends to the correct organization
8. Existing data migration: current data uses user.id as organization_id

## Design Considerations

### Migration Strategy
- Existing ClickHouse data has `organization_id = user.id`
- When adding org support, each existing user needs a "personal" organization created
- If we can create orgs with `id = user.id`, existing ClickHouse data maps naturally without any ClickHouse migration
- Alternative: create orgs with auto-generated IDs and run a ClickHouse UPDATE to remap

### CLI Per-Repository Config
- The `enable` command should store the selected org ID somewhere accessible to the session-end hook
- Options: `.rudel.json` in project root, or extend `~/.rudel/credentials.json` with org mappings
- The session-end hook receives `cwd`, so it can read a project-local file
- `.rudel.json` in project root is cleanest (can be .gitignored)

### API Changes
- `IngestSessionInput` needs an `organizationId` field
- API must verify the authenticated user is a member of the specified organization
- The `authMiddleware` or a new `orgMiddleware` should extract `activeOrganizationId` from the session
- Analytics handlers should use `session.activeOrganizationId` instead of `user.id`

### Web Changes
- Org switcher dropdown in sidebar (top area, replacing or above the "Rudel Analytics" header)
- Organization management page (members list, invite form, settings)
- Accept invitation page
- Create organization flow (for first-time users or adding new orgs)

Design a comprehensive implementation plan for adding multi-tenancy (team/organization support) to the Rudel app. Here's the full context:

## Current Architecture

### Auth (better-auth)
- **Server**: `apps/api/src/auth.ts` - Uses `betterAuth()` with `bearer()` plugin only
- **Client**: `apps/web/src/lib/auth-client.ts` - `createAuthClient({ baseURL: "" })`
- **Postgres schema**: `packages/sql-schema/src/auth-schema.ts` - Only `user`, `session`, `account`, `verification` tables
- No organization/team tables exist in Postgres

### ClickHouse
- `organization_id` is already the **first ORDER BY column** in both `claude_sessions` and `session_analytics`
- Currently `organization_id = user.id` (set in `apps/api/src/router.ts` line 27)
- All 50+ analytics service functions accept `orgId` as first param and filter by `WHERE organization_id = '${org}'`
- No ClickHouse schema changes needed

### API
- `apps/api/src/router.ts` - Sets `organizationId: context.user.id` on ingest
- `apps/api/src/middleware.ts` - `authMiddleware` checks `context.user` and `context.session`, exposes `AppContext { user, session }`
- All analytics handlers pass `context.user.id` as orgId to service functions
- Contract in `packages/api-routes/src/index.ts` - no org params in any route inputs

### CLI
- `apps/cli/src/commands/enable.ts` - Adds session-end hook, no org selection
- `apps/cli/src/commands/hooks/claude/session-end.ts` - Reads stdin `{session_id, transcript_path, cwd}`, uploads with Bearer token
- `apps/cli/src/lib/credentials.ts` - Stores `{token, apiBaseUrl}` in `~/.rudel/credentials.json`
- `apps/cli/src/lib/uploader.ts` - Calls `client.ingestSession(request)` via oRPC
- `apps/cli/src/lib/types.ts` - `IngestRequest` has no `organizationId` field

### Web
- `apps/web/src/components/analytics/Sidebar.tsx` - Nav sidebar with user avatar, no org switcher
- Router in `apps/web/src/App.tsx`
- Analytics pages use `orpc.analytics.*` hooks

## better-auth Organization Plugin (researched)
The `organization` plugin from `better-auth/plugins` provides:
- **Tables**: `organization` (id, name, slug, logo, metadata), `member` (id, organizationId, userId, role), `invitation` (id, organizationId, email, role, status, expiresAt, inviterId)
- **Session extension**: Adds `activeOrganizationId` column to `session` table
- **Roles**: Built-in `owner`, `admin`, `member` roles with configurable access control
- **Server API**: `auth.api.getFullOrganization()`, `auth.api.getActiveMember()`, etc.
- **Client API**: `authClient.organization.create()`, `.setActive()`, `.inviteMember()`, `.listOrganizations()`, etc.
- **React hooks**: `authClient.useActiveOrganization()`, `authClient.useListOrganizations()`
- Invitation flow: create invitation -> send email callback -> user accepts via `acceptInvitation({invitationId})`

## Requirements
1. Every user can be in multiple organizations
2. Every organization can have multiple users
3. Users can be invited via link/invite code, or admin can add by email
4. Organization dropdown in sidebar top-left for switching
5. All analytics are scoped to the active organization
6. CLI `enable` command asks which organization to upload sessions for (per-repository config)
7. Session-end hook sends to the correct organization
8. Existing data migration: current data uses user.id as organization_id

### Migration Strategy
- Existing ClickHouse data has `organization_id = user.id`
- When adding org support, each existing user needs a "personal" organization created
- If we can create orgs with `id = user.id`, existing ClickHouse data maps naturally without any ClickHouse migration
- Alternative: create orgs with auto-generated IDs and run a ClickHouse UPDATE to remap

### CLI Per-Repository Config
- The `enable` command should store the selected org ID somewhere accessible to the session-end hook
- Options: `.rudel.json` in project root, or extend `~/.rudel/credentials.json` with org mappings
- The session-end hook receives `cwd`, so it can read a project-local file
- `.rudel.json` in project root is cleanest (can be .gitignored)

### API Changes
- `IngestSessionInput` needs an `organizationId` field
- API must verify the authenticated user is a member of the specified organization
- The `authMiddleware` or a new `orgMiddleware` should extract `activeOrganizationId` from the session
- Analytics handlers should use `session.activeOrganizationId` instead of `user.id`

### Web Changes
- Org switcher dropdown in sidebar (top area, replacing or above the "Rudel Analytics" header)
- Organization management page (members list, invite form, settings)
- Accept invitation page
- Create organization flow (for first-time users or adding new orgs)

- All analytics handlers pass `context.user.id` as orgId to service functions
- Contract in `packages/api-routes/src/index.ts` - no org params in any route inputs

### CLI
- `apps/cli/src/commands/enable.ts` - Adds session-end hook, no org selection
- `apps/cli/src/commands/hooks/claude/session-end.ts` - Reads stdin `{session_id, transcript_path, cwd}`, uploads with Bearer token
- `apps/cli/src/lib/credentials.ts` - Stores `{token, apiBaseUrl}` in `~/.rudel/credentials.json`
- `apps/cli/src/lib/uploader.ts` - Calls `client.ingestSession(request)` via oRPC
- `apps/cli/src/lib/types.ts` - `IngestRequest` has no `organizationId` field

### Web
- `apps/web/src/components/analytics/Sidebar.tsx` - Nav sidebar with user avatar, no org switcher
- Router in `apps/web/src/App.tsx`
- Analytics pages use `orpc.analytics.*` hooks

## better-auth Organization Plugin (researched)
The `organization` plugin from `better-auth/plugins` provides:
- **Tables**: `organization` (id, name, slug, logo, metadata), `member` (id, organizationId, userId, role), `invitation` (id, organizationId, email, role, status, expiresAt, inviterId)
- **Session extension**: Adds `activeOrganizationId` column to `session` table
- **Roles**: Built-in `owner`, `admin`, `member` roles with configurable access control
- **Server API**: `auth.api.getFullOrganization()`, `auth.api.getActiveMember()`, etc.
- **Client API**: `authClient.organization.create()`, `.setActive()`, `.inviteMember()`, `.listOrganizations()`, etc.
- **React hooks**: `authClient.useActiveOrganization()`, `authClient.useListOrganizations()`
- Invitation flow: create invitation -> send email callback -> user accepts via `acceptInvitation({invitationId})`

## Requirements
1. Every user can be in multiple organizations
2. Every organization can have multiple users
3. Users can be invited via link/invite code, or admin can add by email
4. Organization dropdown in sidebar top-left for switching
5. All analytics are scoped to the active organization
6. CLI `enable` command asks which organization to upload sessions for (per-repository config)
7. Session-end hook sends to the correct organization
8. Existing data migration: current data uses user.id as organization_id

## Design Considerations

### Migration Strategy
- Existing ClickHouse data has `organization_id = user.id`
- When adding org support, each existing user needs a "personal" organization created
- If we can create orgs with `id = user.id`, existing ClickHouse data maps naturally without any ClickHouse migration
- Alternative: create orgs with auto-generated IDs and run a ClickHouse UPDATE to remap

### CLI Per-Repository Config
- The `enable` command should store the selected org ID somewhere accessible to the session-end hook
- Options: `.rudel.json` in project root, or extend `~/.rudel/credentials.json` with org mappings
- The session-end hook receives `cwd`, so it can read a project-local file
- `.rudel.json` in project root is cleanest (can be .gitignored)

### API Changes
- `IngestSessionInput` needs an `organizationId` field
- API must verify the authenticated user is a member of the specified organization
- The `authMiddleware` or a new `orgMiddleware` should extract `activeOrganizationId` from the session
- Analytics handlers should use `session.activeOrganizationId` instead of `user.id`

### Web Changes
- Org switcher dropdown in sidebar (top area, replacing or above the "Rudel Analytics" header)
- Organization management page (members list, invite form, settings)
- Accept invitation page
- Create organization flow (for first-time users or adding new orgs)

Design a comprehensive implementation plan for adding multi-tenancy (team/organization support) to the Rudel app. Here's the full context:

## Current Architecture

### Auth (better-auth)
- **Server**: `apps/api/src/auth.ts` - Uses `betterAuth()` with `bearer()` plugin only
- **Client**: `apps/web/src/lib/auth-client.ts` - `createAuthClient({ baseURL: "" })`
- **Postgres schema**: `packages/sql-schema/src/auth-schema.ts` - Only `user`, `session`, `account`, `verification` tables
- No organization/team tables exist in Postgres

### ClickHouse
- `organization_id` is already the **first ORDER BY column** in both `claude_sessions` and `session_analytics`
- Currently `organization_id = user.id` (set in `apps/api/src/router.ts` line 27)
- All 50+ analytics service functions accept `orgId` as first param and filter by `WHERE organization_id = '${org}'`
- No ClickHouse schema changes needed

### API
- `apps/api/src/router.ts` - Sets `organizationId: context.user.id` on ingest
- `apps/api/src/middleware.ts` - `authMiddleware` checks `context.user` and `context.session`, exposes `AppContext { user, session }`
- All analytics handlers pass `context.user.id` as orgId to service functions
- Contract in `packages/api-routes/src/index.ts` - no org params in any route inputs

### CLI
- `apps/cli/src/commands/enable.ts` - Adds session-end hook, no org selection
- `apps/cli/src/commands/hooks/claude/session-end.ts` - Reads stdin `{session_id, transcript_path, cwd}`, uploads with Bearer token
- `apps/cli/src/lib/credentials.ts` - Stores `{token, apiBaseUrl}` in `~/.rudel/credentials.json`
- `apps/cli/src/lib/uploader.ts` - Calls `client.ingestSession(request)` via oRPC
- `apps/cli/src/lib/types.ts` - `IngestRequest` has no `organizationId` field

### Web
- `apps/web/src/components/analytics/Sidebar.tsx` - Nav sidebar with user avatar, no org switcher
- Router in `apps/web/src/App.tsx`
- Analytics pages use `orpc.analytics.*` hooks

## better-auth Organization Plugin (researched)
The `organization` plugin from `better-auth/plugins` provides:
- **Tables**: `organization` (id, name, slug, logo, metadata), `member` (id, organizationId, userId, role), `invitation` (id, organizationId, email, role, status, expiresAt, inviterId)

- **Roles**: Built-in `owner`, `admin`, `member` roles with configurable access control
- **Server API**: `auth.api.getFullOrganization()`, `auth.api.getActiveMember()`, etc.
- **Client API**: `authClient.organization.create()`, `.setActive()`, `.inviteMember()`, `.listOrganizations()`, etc.
- **React hooks**: `authClient.useActiveOrganization()`, `authClient.useListOrganizations()`
- Invitation flow: create invitation -> send email callback -> user accepts via `acceptInvitation({invitationId})`

## Requirements
1. Every user can be in multiple organizations
2. Every organization can have multiple users
3. Users can be invited via link/invite code, or admin can add by email

5. All analytics are scoped to the active organization

7. Session-end hook sends to the correct organization
8. Existing data migration: current data uses user.id as organization_id

### Migration Strategy
- Existing ClickHouse data has `organization_id = user.id`
- When adding org support, each existing user needs a "personal" organization created
- If we can create orgs with `id = user.id`, existing ClickHouse data maps naturally without any ClickHouse migration

- The `enable` command should store the selected org ID somewhere accessible to the session-end hook
- Options: `.rudel.json` in project root, or extend `~/.rudel/credentials.json` with org mappings
- The session-end hook receives `cwd`, so it can read a project-local file
- `.rudel.json` in project root is cleanest (can be .gitignored)

### API Changes
- `IngestSessionInput` needs an `organizationId` field
- API must verify the authenticated user is a member of the specified organization
- The `authMiddleware` or a new `orgMiddleware` should extract `activeOrganizationId` from the session
- Analytics handlers should use `session.activeOrganizationId` instead of `user.id`

### Web Changes
- Org switcher dropdown in sidebar (top area, replacing or above the "Rudel Analytics" header)
- Organization management page (members list, invite form, settings)
- Accept invitation page
- Create organization flow (for first-time users or adding new orgs)

Design a comprehensive implementation plan for adding multi-tenancy (team/organization support) to the Rudel app. Here's the full context:

## Current Architecture

### Auth (better-auth)
- **Server**: `apps/api/src/auth.ts` - Uses `betterAuth()` with `bearer()` plugin only
- **Client**: `apps/web/src/lib/auth-client.ts` - `createAuthClient({ baseURL: "" })`
- **Postgres schema**: `packages/sql-schema/src/auth-schema.ts` - Only `user`, `session`, `account`, `verification` tables
- No organization/team tables exist in Postgres

### ClickHouse
- `organization_id` is already the **first ORDER BY column** in both `claude_sessions` and `session_analytics`
- Currently `organization_id = user.id` (set in `apps/api/src/router.ts` line 27)
- All 50+ analytics service functions accept `orgId` as first param and filter by `WHERE organization_id = '${org}'`
- No ClickHouse schema changes needed

### API
- `apps/api/src/router.ts` - Sets `organizationId: context.user.id` on ingest
- `apps/api/src/middleware.ts` - `authMiddleware` checks `context.user` and `context.session`, exposes `AppContext { user, session }`
- All analytics handlers pass `context.user.id` as orgId to service functions
- Contract in `packages/api-routes/src/index.ts` - no org params in any route inputs

### CLI
- `apps/cli/src/commands/enable.ts` - Adds session-end hook, no org selection
- `apps/cli/src/commands/hooks/claude/session-end.ts` - Reads stdin `{session_id, transcript_path, cwd}`, uploads with Bearer token
- `apps/cli/src/lib/credentials.ts` - Stores `{token, apiBaseUrl}` in `~/.rudel/credentials.json`
- `apps/cli/src/lib/uploader.ts` - Calls `client.ingestSession(request)` via oRPC
- `apps/cli/src/lib/types.ts` - `IngestRequest` has no `organizationId` field

### Web
- `apps/web/src/components/analytics/Sidebar.tsx` - Nav sidebar with user avatar, no org switcher
- Router in `apps/web/src/App.tsx`
- Analytics pages use `orpc.analytics.*` hooks

## better-auth Organization Plugin (researched)
The `organization` plugin from `better-auth/plugins` provides:
- **Tables**: `organization` (id, name, slug, logo, metadata), `member` (id, organizationId, userId, role), `invitation` (id, organizationId, email, role, status, expiresAt, inviterId)
- **Session extension**: Adds `activeOrganizationId` column to `session` table
- **Roles**: Built-in `owner`, `admin`, `member` roles with configurable access control
- **Server API**: `auth.api.getFullOrganization()`, `auth.api.getActiveMember()`, etc.
- **Client API**: `authClient.organization.create()`, `.setActive()`, `.inviteMember()`, `.listOrganizations()`, etc.
- **React hooks**: `authClient.useActiveOrganization()`, `authClient.useListOrganizations()`
- Invitation flow: create invitation -> send email callback -> user accepts via `acceptInvitation({invitationId})`

## Requirements
1. Every user can be in multiple organizations
2. Every organization can have multiple users
3. Users can be invited via link/invite code, or admin can add by email

5. All analytics are scoped to the active organization

- `IngestSessionInput` needs an `organizationId` field

- The `authMiddleware` or a new `orgMiddleware` should extract `activeOrganizationId` from the session
- Analytics handlers should use `session.activeOrganizationId` instead of `user.id`

### Web Changes
- Org switcher dropdown in sidebar (top area, replacing or above the "Rudel Analytics" header)
- Organization management page (members list, invite form, settings)
- Accept invitation page
- Create organization flow (for first-time users or adding new orgs)

data.prompt:
Design a comprehensive implementation plan for adding multi-tenancy (team/organization support) to the Rudel app. Here's the full context:

## Current Architecture

### Auth (better-auth)
- **Server**: `apps/api/src/auth.ts` - Uses `betterAuth()` with `bearer()` plugin only
- **Client**: `apps/web/src/lib/auth-client.ts` - `createAuthClient({ baseURL: "" })`
- **Postgres schema**: `packages/sql-schema/src/auth-schema.ts` - Only `user`, `session`, `account`, `verification` tables
- No organization/team tables exist in Postgres

### ClickHouse
- `organization_id` is already the **first ORDER BY column** in both `claude_sessions` and `session_analytics`
- Currently `organization_id = user.id` (set in `apps/api/src/router.ts` line 27)
- All 50+ analytics service functions accept `orgId` as first param and filter by `WHERE organization_id = '${org}'`
- No ClickHouse schema changes needed

### API
- `apps/api/src/router.ts` - Sets `organizationId: context.user.id` on ingest
- `apps/api/src/middleware.ts` - `authMiddleware` checks `context.user` and `context.session`, exposes `AppContext { user, session }`
- All analytics handlers pass `context.user.id` as orgId to service functions
- Contract in `packages/api-routes/src/index.ts` - no org params in any route inputs

### CLI
- `apps/cli/src/commands/enable.ts` - Adds session-end hook, no org selection
- `apps/cli/src/commands/hooks/claude/session-end.ts` - Reads stdin `{session_id, transcript_path, cwd}`, uploads with Bearer token
- `apps/cli/src/lib/credentials.ts` - Stores `{token, apiBaseUrl}` in `~/.rudel/credentials.json`
- `apps/cli/src/lib/uploader.ts` - Calls `client.ingestSession(request)` via oRPC
- `apps/cli/src/lib/types.ts` - `IngestRequest` has no `organizationId` field

### Web
- `apps/web/src/components/analytics/Sidebar.tsx` - Nav sidebar with user avatar, no org switcher
- Router in `apps/web/src/App.tsx`
- Analytics pages use `orpc.analytics.*` hooks

## better-auth Organization Plugin (researched)
The `organization` plugin from `better-auth/plugins` provides:
- **Tables**: `organization` (id, name, slug, logo, metadata), `member` (id, organizationId, userId, role), `invitation` (id, organizationId, email, role, status, expiresAt, inviterId)
- **Session extension**: Adds `activeOrganizationId` column to `session` table
- **Roles**: Built-in `owner`, `admin`, `member` roles with configurable access control
- **Server API**: `auth.api.getFullOrganization()`, `auth.api.getActiveMember()`, etc.
- **Client API**: `authClient.organization.create()`, `.setActive()`, `.inviteMember()`, `.listOrganizations()`, etc.
- **React hooks**: `authClient.useActiveOrganization()`, `authClient.useListOrganizations()`
- Invitation flow: create invitation -> send email callback -> user accepts via `acceptInvitation({invitationId})`

## Requirements
1. Every user can be in multiple organizations
2. Every organization can have multiple users
3. Users can be invited via link/invite code, or admin can add by email
4. Organization dropdown in sidebar top-left for switching
5. All analytics are scoped to the active organization
6. CLI `enable` command asks which organization to upload sessions for (per-repository config)
7. Session-end hook sends to the correct organization
8. Existing data migration: current data uses user.id as organization_id

## Design Considerations

### Migration Strategy
- Existing ClickHouse data has `organization_id = user.id`
- When adding org support, each existing user needs a "personal" organization created
- If we can create orgs with `id = user.id`, existing ClickHouse data maps naturally without any ClickHouse migration
- Alternative: create orgs with auto-generated IDs and run a ClickHouse UPDATE to remap

### CLI Per-Repository Config
- The `enable` command should store the selected org ID somewhere accessible to the session-end hook
- Options: `.rudel.json` in project root, or extend `~/.rudel/credentials.json` with org mappings
- The session-end hook receives `cwd`, so it can read a project-local file
- `.rudel.json` in project root is cleanest (can be .gitignored)

### API Changes
- `IngestSessionInput` needs an `organizationId` field
- API must verify the authenticated user is a member of the specified organization
- The `authMiddleware` or a new `orgMiddleware` should extract `activeOrganizationId` from the session
- Analytics handlers should use `session.activeOrganizationId` instead of `user.id`

### Web Changes
- Org switcher dropdown in sidebar (top area, replacing or above the "Rudel Analytics" header)
- Organization management page (members list, invite form, settings)
- Accept invitation page
- Create organization flow (for first-time users or adding new orgs)

Please design a detailed, phased implementation plan with specific file changes, code patterns, and migration strategy. Consider the order of operations carefully - what can be done independently vs what depends on other changes.

## Current Architecture

### Auth (better-auth)
- **Server**: `apps/api/src/auth.ts` - Uses `betterAuth()` with `bearer()` plugin only
- **Client**: `apps/web/src/lib/auth-client.ts` - `createAuthClient({ baseURL: "" })`

- No organization/team tables exist in Postgres

- `organization_id` is already the **first ORDER BY column** in both `claude_sessions` and `session_analytics`
- Currently `organization_id = user.id` (set in `apps/api/src/router.ts` line 27)
- All 50+ analytics service functions accept `orgId` as first param and filter by `WHERE organization_id = '${org}'`
- No ClickHouse schema changes needed

### API
- `apps/api/src/router.ts` - Sets `organizationId: context.user.id` on ingest
- `apps/api/src/middleware.ts` - `authMiddleware` checks `context.user` and `context.session`, exposes `AppContext { user, session }`

- `apps/cli/src/commands/enable.ts` - Adds session-end hook, no org selection
- `apps/cli/src/commands/hooks/claude/session-end.ts` - Reads stdin `{session_id, transcript_path, cwd}`, uploads with Bearer token
- `apps/cli/src/lib/credentials.ts` - Stores `{token, apiBaseUrl}` in `~/.rudel/credentials.json`
- `apps/cli/src/lib/uploader.ts` - Calls `client.ingestSession(request)` via oRPC
- `apps/cli/src/lib/types.ts` - `IngestRequest` has no `organizationId` field

### Web
- `apps/web/src/components/analytics/Sidebar.tsx` - Nav sidebar with user avatar, no org switcher
- Router in `apps/web/src/App.tsx`
- Analytics pages use `orpc.analytics.*` hooks

The `organization` plugin from `better-auth/plugins` provides:
- **Tables**: `organization` (id, name, slug, logo, metadata), `member` (id, organizationId, userId, role), `invitation` (id, organizationId, email, role, status, expiresAt, inviterId)

- **React hooks**: `authClient.useActiveOrganization()`, `authClient.useListOrganizations()`
- Invitation flow: create invitation -> send email callback -> user accepts via `acceptInvitation({invitationId})`

## Requirements
1. Every user can be in multiple organizations
2. Every organization can have multiple users

- The session-end hook receives `cwd`, so it can read a project-local file
- `.rudel.json` in project root is cleanest (can be .gitignored)

- `IngestSessionInput` needs an `organizationId` field
- API must verify the authenticated user is a member of the specified organization
- The `authMiddleware` or a new `orgMiddleware` should extract `activeOrganizationId` from the session
- Analytics handlers should use `session.activeOrganizationId` instead of `user.id`

- Organization management page (members list, invite form, settings)
- Accept invitation page
- Create organization flow (for first-time users or adding new orgs)

content.text:
### Configure BetterAuth Client with Organization Plugin (TypeScript)

Source: https://www.better-auth.com/llms.txt/docs/plugins/organization

This snippet illustrates how to integrate the organization client plugin into your `better-auth/client` configuration file (`auth-client.ts`). It imports `organizationClient` and adds it to the `plugins` array during `createAuthClient` initialization, enabling client-side organization management features.

```ts
import { createAuthClient } from "better-auth/client"
import { organizationClient } from "better-auth/client/plugins"

export const authClient = createAuthClient({
    plugins: [ // [!code highlight]
        organizationClient() // [!code highlight]
    ] // [!code highlight]
})
```

--------------------------------

### Set Active Organization with BetterAuth Client-Side API (TypeScript)

Source: https://www.better-auth.com/llms.txt/docs/plugins/organization

This code demonstrates how to set the active organization for the current user session on the client side using `authClient.organization.setActive`. It allows specifying the organization by `organizationId` or `organizationSlug`, and can also unset the active organization by passing `null`.

```ts
const { data, error } = await authClient.organization.setActive({
    organizationId: org-id, // optional
    organizationSlug: org-slug, // optional
});
```

--------------------------------

### POST /api/organization/setActive

Source: https://www.better-auth.com/llms.txt/docs/plugins/organization

Sets the active organization for the authenticated user's session. The active organization represents the workspace the user is currently working on. Can be called with either organizationId or organizationSlug, and supports unsetting the active organization by passing null.

```APIDOC
## POST /api/organization/setActive

### Description
Sets the active organization for the user's session. The active organization represents the current workspace context. Supports setting by organization ID or slug, and can be unset by passing null.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/api/organization/setActive

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **organizationId** (string | null) - Optional - The organization ID to set as active. Pass null to unset the active organization
- **organizationSlug** (string) - Optional - The organization slug to set as active. Used if organizationId is not provided

### Authentication
Requires valid session cookies

### Request Example
**Client Side:**
```ts
const { data, error } = await authClient.organization.setActive({
  organizationId: "org-123",
  organizationSlug: "acme-corp"
})
```

**Server Side:**
```ts
const data = await auth.api.setActiveOrganization({
  body: {
    organizationId: "org-123",
    organizationSlug: "acme-corp"
  },
  headers: await headers()
})
```

**Unset Active Organization:**
```ts
const { data, error } = await authClient.organization.setActive({
  organizationId: null
})
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **activeOrganizationId** (string | null) - The newly set active organization ID, or null if unset
- **success** (boolean) - Indicates successful operation

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "activeOrganizationId": "org-123",
  "success": true
}
```

### Error Handling
- **400 Bad Request** - Invalid organizationId or organizationSlug provided
- **401 Unauthorized** - No valid session found
- **404 Not Found** - Organization not found or user is not a member
- **500 Internal Server Error** - Server-side error setting active organization

### Notes
- By default, the active organization is set to null when a user signs in
- The active organization can be managed client-side only, allowing different tabs to have different active organizations
- Use database hooks to automatically set an initial active organization on session creation
```

### Organizations

Source: https://www.better-auth.com/llms.txt/docs/plugins/oauth-provider

OAuth Clients are tied to either a user or `reference_id` at registration and is immutable. If you are utilizing the [organization plugin](/docs/plugins/organization), you must ensure that the [`activeOrganizationId`](/docs/plugins/organization#active-organization) is set on your active session when you create new clients. To set user-specific permissions and roles on tokens see [Claims](#claims).

--------------------------------

### Organizations > List User's Organizations

Source: https://www.better-auth.com/llms.txt/docs/plugins/organization

To list the organizations that a user is a member of, you can use the `useListOrganizations` hook, which implements a reactive way to get the organizations that the user is a member of. This hook is available across multiple frameworks including React, Vue, and Svelte, allowing you to display organization data in a framework-agnostic manner. Alternatively, if you prefer not to use a hook, you can call `organization.list` directly on the client side or use `auth.api.listOrganizations` on the server side with appropriate session cookies.

data.prompt:
Design a comprehensive implementation plan for adding multi-tenancy (team/organization support) to the Rudel app. Here's the full context:

## Current Architecture

### Auth (better-auth)
- **Server**: `apps/api/src/auth.ts` - Uses `betterAuth()` with `bearer()` plugin only
- **Client**: `apps/web/src/lib/auth-client.ts` - `createAuthClient({ baseURL: "" })`
- **Postgres schema**: `packages/sql-schema/src/auth-schema.ts` - Only `user`, `session`, `account`, `verification` tables
- No organization/team tables exist in Postgres

### ClickHouse
- `organization_id` is already the **first ORDER BY column** in both `claude_sessions` and `session_analytics`
- Currently `organization_id = user.id` (set in `apps/api/src/router.ts` line 27)
- All 50+ analytics service functions accept `orgId` as first param and filter by `WHERE organization_id = '${org}'`
- No ClickHouse schema changes needed

### API
- `apps/api/src/router.ts` - Sets `organizationId: context.user.id` on ingest
- `apps/api/src/middleware.ts` - `authMiddleware` checks `context.user` and `context.session`, exposes `AppContext { user, session }`
- All analytics handlers pass `context.user.id` as orgId to service functions
- Contract in `packages/api-routes/src/index.ts` - no org params in any route inputs

### CLI
- `apps/cli/src/commands/enable.ts` - Adds session-end hook, no org selection
- `apps/cli/src/commands/hooks/claude/session-end.ts` - Reads stdin `{session_id, transcript_path, cwd}`, uploads with Bearer token
- `apps/cli/src/lib/credentials.ts` - Stores `{token, apiBaseUrl}` in `~/.rudel/credentials.json`
- `apps/cli/src/lib/uploader.ts` - Calls `client.ingestSession(request)` via oRPC
- `apps/cli/src/lib/types.ts` - `IngestRequest` has no `organizationId` field

### Web
- `apps/web/src/components/analytics/Sidebar.tsx` - Nav sidebar with user avatar, no org switcher
- Router in `apps/web/src/App.tsx`
- Analytics pages use `orpc.analytics.*` hooks

## better-auth Organization Plugin (researched)
The `organization` plugin from `better-auth/plugins` provides:
- **Tables**: `organization` (id, name, slug, logo, metadata), `member` (id, organizationId, userId, role), `invitation` (id, organizationId, email, role, status, expiresAt, inviterId)
- **Session extension**: Adds `activeOrganizationId` column to `session` table
- **Roles**: Built-in `owner`, `admin`, `member` roles with configurable access control
- **Server API**: `auth.api.getFullOrganization()`, `auth.api.getActiveMember()`, etc.
- **Client API**: `authClient.organization.create()`, `.setActive()`, `.inviteMember()`, `.listOrganizations()`, etc.
- **React hooks**: `authClient.useActiveOrganization()`, `authClient.useListOrganizations()`
- Invitation flow: create invitation -> send email callback -> user accepts via `acceptInvitation({invitationId})`

## Requirements
1. Every user can be in multiple organizations
2. Every organization can have multiple users
3. Users can be invited via link/invite code, or admin can add by email
4. Organization dropdown in sidebar top-left for switching
5. All analytics are scoped to the active organization
6. CLI `enable` command asks which organization to upload sessions for (per-repository config)
7. Session-end hook sends to the correct organization
8. Existing data migration: current data uses user.id as organization_id

## Design Considerations

### Migration Strategy
- Existing ClickHouse data has `organization_id = user.id`
- When adding org support, each existing user needs a "personal" organization created
- If we can create orgs with `id = user.id`, existing ClickHouse data maps naturally without any ClickHouse migration
- Alternative: create orgs with auto-generated IDs and run a ClickHouse UPDATE to remap

### CLI Per-Repository Config
- The `enable` command should store the selected org ID somewhere accessible to the session-end hook
- Options: `.rudel.json` in project root, or extend `~/.rudel/credentials.json` with org mappings
- The session-end hook receives `cwd`, so it can read a project-local file
- `.rudel.json` in project root is cleanest (can be .gitignored)

### API Changes
- `IngestSessionInput` needs an `organizationId` field
- API must verify the authenticated user is a member of the specified organization
- The `authMiddleware` or a new `orgMiddleware` should extract `activeOrganizationId` from the session
- Analytics handlers should use `session.activeOrganizationId` instead of `user.id`

### Web Changes
- Org switcher dropdown in sidebar (top area, replacing or above the "Rudel Analytics" header)
- Organization management page (members list, invite form, settings)
- Accept invitation page
- Create organization flow (for first-time users or adding new orgs)

Please design a detailed, phased implementation plan with specific file changes, code patterns, and migration strategy. Consider the order of operations carefully - what can be done independently vs what depends on other changes.

content.content:
1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→set -e
     3→
     4→# 1. Start Postgres + ClickHouse (idempotent)
     5→docker compose up -d --wait
     6→
     7→# 2. Run Postgres migrations (idempotent — skips already-applied)
     8→PG_CONNECTION_STRING=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/rudel \
     9→  bun run --cwd packages/sql-schema migrate
    10→
    11→# 3. Start API + Web in parallel with local env vars
    12→export PG_CONNECTION_STRING=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/rudel
    13→export BETTER_AUTH_SECRET=local-dev-secret-that-is-at-least-32-chars-long
    14→export CLICKHOUSE_URL=http://localhost:8123
    15→export REDACTED
    16→export APP_URL=http://localhost:4011
    17→
    18→# Run API and Web in parallel, kill both on Ctrl+C
    19→bun --watch apps/api/src/index.ts &
    20→API_PID=$!
    21→bun run --cwd apps/web dev &
    22→WEB_PID=$!
    23→
    24→trap "kill $API_PID $WEB_PID 2>/dev/null" EXIT
    25→wait
    26→

1→services:
     2→  postgres:
     3→    image: postgres:16-alpine
     4→    ports:
     5→      - "5432:5432"
     6→    environment:
     7→      POSTGRES_USER: postgres
     8→      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres
     9→      POSTGRES_DB: rudel
    10→    volumes:
    11→      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    12→    healthcheck:
    13→      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U postgres"]
    14→      interval: 5s
    15→      timeout: 3s
    16→      retries: 5
    17→
    18→  clickhouse:
    19→    image: clickhouse/clickhouse-server:latest
    20→    ports:
    21→      - "8123:8123"
    22→    environment:
    23→      CLICKHOUSE_PASSWORD: clickhouse
    24→    volumes:
    25→      - clickhouse_data:/var/lib/clickhouse
    26→      - ./scripts/init-clickhouse-local.sql:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init.sql:ro
    27→    healthcheck:
    28→      test: ["CMD", "clickhouse-client", "--password", "clickhouse", "--query", "SELECT 1"]
    29→      interval: 5s
    30→      timeout: 3s
    31→      retries: 5
    32→
    33→volumes:
    34→  postgres_data:
    35→  clickhouse_data:
    36→

- **Client API**: `authClient.organization.create()`, `.setActive()`, `.inviteMember()`, `.listOrganizations()`, etc.
- **React hooks**: `authClient.useActiveOrganization()`, `authClient.useListOrganizations()`
- Invitation flow: create invitation -> send email callback -> user accepts via `acceptInvitation({invitationId})`

## Requirements
1. Every user can be in multiple organizations
2. Every organization can have multiple users
3. Users can be invited via link/invite code, or admin can add by email
4. Organization dropdown in sidebar top-left for switching
5. All analytics are scoped to the active organization
6. CLI `enable` command asks which organization to upload sessions for (per-repository config)
7. Session-end hook sends to the correct organization
8. Existing data migration: current data uses user.id as organization_id

## Design Considerations

### Migration Strategy
- Existing ClickHouse data has `organization_id = user.id`
- When adding org support, each existing user needs a "personal" organization created
- If we can create orgs with `id = user.id`, existing ClickHouse data maps naturally without any ClickHouse migration
- Alternative: create orgs with auto-generated IDs and run a ClickHouse UPDATE to remap

### CLI Per-Repository Config
- The `enable` command should store the selected org ID somewhere accessible to the session-end hook
- Options: `.rudel.json` in project root, or extend `~/.rudel/credentials.json` with org mappings
- The session-end hook receives `cwd`, so it can read a project-local file
- `.rudel.json` in project root is cleanest (can be .gitignored)

### API Changes
- `IngestSessionInput` needs an `organizationId` field
- API must verify the authenticated user is a member of the specified organization
- The `authMiddleware` or a new `orgMiddleware` should extract `activeOrganizationId` from the session
- Analytics handlers should use `session.activeOrganizationId` instead of `user.id`

### Web Changes
- Org switcher dropdown in sidebar (top area, replacing or above the "Rudel Analytics" header)
- Organization management page (members list, invite form, settings)
- Accept invitation page
- Create organization flow (for first-time users or adding new orgs)

Please design a detailed, phased implementation plan with specific file changes, code patterns, and migration strategy. Consider the order of operations carefully - what can be done independently vs what depends on other changes.

Drizzle migrations are SQL files. We could:
1. Add a data migration SQL file that runs after the schema migration
2. Use Drizzle's custom migration feature

Looking at how Drizzle migrations work - they generate SQL files in a migrations folder. We could add a custom SQL migration that:
1. Creates an organization for each existing user with `id = user.id`
2. Creates a member row for each
3. Updates sessions to set `activeOrganizationId`


This would be pure SQL and would run automatically with `drizzle-kit migrate`. I need to check the project's Drizzle configuration and migration setup to see how to implement this properly. The user mentioned they've already completed the schema changes themselves—adding the organization, member, and invitation tables plus the activeOrganizationId field to sessions—so I can focus on the data migration approach.

content.text:
Yes, absolutely. We can make it a dedicated Drizzle migration that runs right after the schema migration. It would be a pure SQL data migration file that:

```sql
-- Insert a personal org for each existing user (id = user.id)
INSERT INTO organization (id, name, slug, created_at)
SELECT id, name || '''s Workspace', lower(replace(split_part(email, '@', 1), '.', '-')), now()
FROM "user"
ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;

-- Make each user the owner of their personal org
INSERT INTO member (id, organization_id, user_id, role, created_at)
SELECT gen_random_uuid()::text, u.id, u.id, 'owner', now()
FROM "user" u
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
  SELECT 1 FROM member m WHERE m.user_id = u.id AND m.organization_id = u.id
);

-- Set activeOrganizationId on existing sessions
UPDATE session SET active_organization_id = user_id
WHERE active_organization_id IS NULL;
```

Drizzle supports custom SQL migration files in the migrations folder. So the flow would be:

1. **Migration 0001** (auto-generated by `drizzle-kit generate`): Creates `organization`, `member`, `invitation` tables + adds `active_organization_id` to `session`
2. **Migration 0002** (hand-written SQL): The data migration above that backfills personal orgs

Both run in order when you do `bun run migrate`. The slug uniqueness edge case (two users with same email prefix) can be handled with a suffix fallback in the SQL.

Want me to proceed with implementing the full plan? I see you've already made the Drizzle schema changes (organization, member, invitation tables + `activeOrganizationId` on session + updated exports). I'll pick up from there.

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have a complete picture. Let me create a comprehensive summary of the Drizzle ORM migration setup.

## Drizzle ORM Migration Setup - Complete Overview

I've thoroughly explored the Drizzle ORM migration configuration in this project. Here's what I found:

### 1. Drizzle Configuration File

**Location:** `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/wellington/packages/sql-schema/drizzle.config.ts`

```typescript
import { defineConfig } from "drizzle-kit";

export default defineConfig({
  schema: "./src/auth-schema.ts",
  out: "./db/migrations",
  dialect: "postgresql",
});
```

Key settings:
- Schema source: `src/auth-schema.ts` (the single source of truth for table definitions)
- Migration output directory: `db/migrations` (relative to package root)
- Database dialect: PostgreSQL

### 2. Package.json Scripts (packages/sql-schema)

**Location:** `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/wellington/packages/sql-schema/package.json`

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "check-types": "tsc --noEmit",
    "drizzle:check": "drizzle-kit check",
    "drizzle:generate": "drizzle-kit generate",
    "migrate": "bun run src/migrate.ts",
    "migrate:ci": "doppler run --project rudel --config ci -- bun run src/migrate.ts",
    "migrate:prd": "doppler run --project rudel --config prd -- bun run src/migrate.ts"
  }
}
```

Scripts breakdown:
- `drizzle:check`: Verify schema against database
- `drizzle:generate`: Generate new migrations from schema changes
- `migrate`: Run migrations locally (requires PG_CONNECTION_STRING env var)
- `migrate:ci`: Run migrations in CI environment (with Doppler secrets)
- `migrate:prd`: Run migrations in production (with Doppler secrets)

### 3. Migration Execution Script

**Location:** `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/wellington/packages/sql-schema/src/migrate.ts`

```typescript
import { join } from "node:path";
import { drizzle } from "drizzle-orm/postgres-js";
import { migrate } from "drizzle-orm/postgres-js/migrator";
import postgres from "postgres";

const connectionString = process.env.PG_CONNECTION_STRING;
if (!connectionString) {
  throw new Error("PG_CONNECTION_STRING environment variable is required");
}

const sql = postgres(connectionString, { max: 1 });
const db = drizzle(sql);

const migrationsFolder = join(import.meta.dir, "..", "db", "migrations");

console.log("Running migrations...");
await migrate(db, { migrationsFolder });
console.log("Migrations applied successfully.");

await sql.end();
```

How it works:
- Uses `postgres` library for connection pooling (max 1 connection)
- Uses `drizzle-orm/postgres-js/migrator` to apply all pending migrations
- Reads from `db/migrations` folder
- Requires `PG_CONNECTION_STRING` environment variable
- Logs progress and closes connection when done

### 4. Existing Migration Files

**Migrations folder:** `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/wellington/packages/sql-schema/db/migrations/`

Two migration files exist:

#### Migration 0: `0000_supreme_texas_twister.sql`
Creates initial auth tables:
- `user` table (id, name, email, email_verified, image, created_at, updated_at)
- `session` table (id, expires_at, token, user_id with FK, ip_address, user_agent, created_at, updated_at)
- `account` table (id, account_id, provider_id, user_id with FK, OAuth token fields, created_at, updated_at)
- `verification` table (id, identifier, value, expires_at, created_at, updated_at)

#### Migration 1: `0001_flippant_sharon_ventura.sql`
Adds multi-tenancy support:
- Creates `organization` table (id, name, slug, logo, metadata, created_at)
- Creates `member` table (id, organization_id with FK, user_id with FK, role, created_at)
- Creates `invitation` table (id, organization_id with FK, email, role, status, expires_at, inviter_id with FK)
- Adds `active_organization_id` column to `session` table

### 5. Migration Metadata

**Journal file:** `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/wellington/packages/sql-schema/db/migrations/meta/_journal.json`

```json
{
  "version": "7",
  "dialect": "postgresql",
  "entries": [
    {
      "idx": 0,
      "version": "7",
      "when": 1771644519976,
      "tag": "0000_supreme_texas_twister",
      "breakpoints": true
    },
    {
      "idx": 1,
      "version": "7",
      "when": 1771904565586,
      "tag": "0001_flippant_sharon_ventura",
      "breakpoints": true
    }
  ]
}
```

Tracks migration history with timestamps (when applied).

**Snapshot files:** `0000_snapshot.json` and `0001_snapshot.json`
- Store schema state after each migration
- Used by drizzle-kit to detect schema drift

### 6. Schema Definition

**Location:** `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/wellington/packages/sql-schema/src/auth-schema.ts`

The TypeScript schema defines 7 Drizzle tables:
- `user` with indexes on email
- `session` with FK to user, unique constraint on token
- `account` with FK to user
- `verification`
- `organization` with unique constraint on slug
- `member` with FKs to organization and user
- `invitation` with FKs to organization and user

All tables use text IDs, timestamps with timezone, and cascading deletes on foreign keys.

### 7. Root package.json

**Location:** `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/wellington/package.json`

No direct migration scripts at root level, but note:
- Uses workspace setup with `packages/*` and `apps/*`
- No global migration command (migrations are invoked per-package)
- Includes `dev:local` script that runs docker compose (which likely triggers local migrations)

### Migration Workflow Summary

1. **Modify schema:** Edit `/packages/sql-schema/src/auth-schema.ts`
2. **Generate migration:** Run `bun run --cwd packages/sql-schema drizzle:generate`
3. **Review:** Check generated SQL in `/packages/sql-schema/db/migrations/`
4. **Apply locally:** Run `bun run --cwd packages/sql-schema migrate`
5. **Apply in CI/PRD:** Run `bun run --cwd packages/sql-schema migrate:ci` or `migrate:prd`

All migration files must reside in the `db/migrations/` directory with sequential numeric prefixes.

1→import { boolean, pgTable, text, timestamp } from "drizzle-orm/pg-core";
     2→
     3→export const user = pgTable("user", {
     4→	id: text("id").primaryKey(),
     5→	name: text("name").notNull(),
     6→	email: text("email").notNull().unique(),
     7→	emailVerified: boolean("email_verified").default(false).notNull(),
     8→	image: text("image"),
     9→	createdAt: timestamp("created_at", { withTimezone: true, mode: "date" })
    10→		.defaultNow()
    11→		.notNull(),
    12→	updatedAt: timestamp("updated_at", { withTimezone: true, mode: "date" })
    13→		.defaultNow()
    14→		.$onUpdate(() => new Date())
    15→		.notNull(),
    16→});
    17→
    18→export const session = pgTable("session", {
    19→	id: text("id").primaryKey(),
    20→	expiresAt: timestamp("expires_at", {
    21→		withTimezone: true,
    22→		mode: "date",
    23→	}).notNull(),
    24→	token: text("token").notNull().unique(),
    25→	createdAt: timestamp("created_at", { withTimezone: true, mode: "date" })
    26→		.defaultNow()
    27→		.notNull(),
    28→	updatedAt: timestamp("updated_at", { withTimezone: true, mode: "date" })
    29→		.defaultNow()
    30→		.$onUpdate(() => new Date())
    31→		.notNull(),
    32→	ipAddress: text("ip_address"),
    33→	userAgent: text("user_agent"),
    34→	userId: text("user_id")
    35→		.notNull()
    36→		.references(() => user.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
    37→	activeOrganizationId: text("active_organization_id"),
    38→});
    39→
    40→export const account = pgTable("account", {
    41→	id: text("id").primaryKey(),
    42→	accountId: text("account_id").notNull(),
    43→	providerId: text("provider_id").notNull(),
    44→	userId: text("user_id")
    45→		.notNull()
    46→		.references(() => user.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
    47→	accessToken: text("access_token"),
    48→	refreshToken: text("refresh_token"),
    49→	idToken: text("id_token"),
    50→	accessTokenExpiresAt: timestamp("access_token_expires_at", {
    51→		withTimezone: true,
    52→		mode: "date",
    53→	}),
    54→	refreshTokenExpiresAt: timestamp("refresh_token_expires_at", {
    55→		withTimezone: true,
    56→		mode: "date",
    57→	}),
    58→	scope: text("scope"),
    59→	password: text("password"),
    60→	createdAt: timestamp("created_at", { withTimezone: true, mode: "date" })
    61→		.defaultNow()
    62→		.notNull(),
    63→	updatedAt: timestamp("updated_at", { withTimezone: true, mode: "date" })
    64→		.defaultNow()
    65→		.$onUpdate(() => new Date())
    66→		.notNull(),
    67→});
    68→
    69→export const verification = pgTable("verification", {
    70→	id: text("id").primaryKey(),
    71→	identifier: text("identifier").notNull(),
    72→	value: text("value").notNull(),
    73→	expiresAt: timestamp("expires_at", {
    74→		withTimezone: true,
    75→		mode: "date",
    76→	}).notNull(),
    77→	createdAt: timestamp("created_at", { withTimezone: true, mode: "date" })
    78→		.defaultNow()
    79→		.notNull(),
    80→	updatedAt: timestamp("updated_at", { withTimezone: true, mode: "date" })
    81→		.defaultNow()
    82→		.$onUpdate(() => new Date())
    83→		.notNull(),
    84→});
    85→
    86→export const organization = pgTable("organization", {
    87→	id: text("id").primaryKey(),
    88→	name: text("name").notNull(),
    89→	slug: text("slug").notNull().unique(),
    90→	logo: text("logo"),
    91→	metadata: text("metadata"),
    92→	createdAt: timestamp("created_at", { withTimezone: true, mode: "date" })
    93→		.defaultNow()
    94→		.notNull(),
    95→});
    96→
    97→export const member = pgTable("member", {
    98→	id: text("id").primaryKey(),
    99→	organizationId: text("organization_id")
   100→		.notNull()
   101→		.references(() => organization.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
   102→	userId: text("user_id")
   103→		.notNull()
   104→		.references(() => user.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
   105→	role: text("role").notNull().default("member"),
   106→	createdAt: timestamp("created_at", { withTimezone: true, mode: "date" })
   107→		.defaultNow()
   108→		.notNull(),
   109→});
   110→
   111→export const invitation = pgTable("invitation", {
   112→	id: text("id").primaryKey(),
   113→	organizationId: text("organization_id")
   114→		.notNull()
   115→		.references(() => organization.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
   116→	email: text("email").notNull(),
   117→	role: text("role"),
   118→	status: text("status").notNull().default("pending"),
   119→	expiresAt: timestamp("expires_at", {
   120→		withTimezone: true,
   121→		mode: "date",
   122→	}).notNull(),
   123→	inviterId: text("inviter_id")
   124→		.notNull()
   125→		.references(() => user.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
   126→});
   127→
   128→export type UserSelect = typeof user.$inferSelect;
   129→export type UserInsert = typeof user.$inferInsert;
   130→export type SessionSelect = typeof session.$inferSelect;
   131→export type SessionInsert = typeof session.$inferInsert;
   132→export type AccountSelect = typeof account.$inferSelect;
   133→export type AccountInsert = typeof account.$inferInsert;
   134→export type VerificationSelect = typeof verification.$inferSelect;
   135→export type VerificationInsert = typeof verification.$inferInsert;
   136→export type OrganizationSelect = typeof organization.$inferSelect;
   137→export type OrganizationInsert = typeof organization.$inferInsert;
   138→export type MemberSelect = typeof member.$inferSelect;
   139→export type MemberInsert = typeof member.$inferInsert;
   140→export type InvitationSelect = typeof invitation.$inferSelect;
   141→export type InvitationInsert = typeof invitation.$inferInsert;
   142→

import { oc } from "@orpc/contract";
import { z } from "zod";
import {
	DaysInputSchema,
	DeveloperCostBreakdownSchema,
	DeveloperDetailsInputSchema,
	DeveloperDetailsSchema,
	DeveloperErrorSchema,
	DeveloperFeatureUsageSchema,
	DeveloperProjectSchema,
	DeveloperSessionSchema,
	DeveloperSessionsInputSchema,
	DeveloperSummarySchema,
	DeveloperTimelineSchema,
	DeveloperTrendDataPointSchema,
	DimensionAnalysisDataPointSchema,
	DimensionAnalysisInputSchema,
	ErrorTrendDataPointSchema,
	ErrorTrendsInputSchema,
	InsightSchema,
	LearningEntrySchema,
	LearningsFeedStatsSchema,
	LearningsTrendDataPointSchema,
	LearningsTrendInputSchema,
	ModelTokensTrendDataSchema,
	OverviewKPIsSchema,
	PaginatedDaysInputSchema,
	ProjectContributorSchema,
	ProjectCostBreakdownSchema,
	ProjectDetailDataSchema,
	ProjectDetailsInputSchema,
	ProjectErrorSchema,
	ProjectFeatureUsageSchema,
	ProjectInvestmentSchema,
	ProjectTrendDataPointSchema,
	RecurringErrorSchema,
	RecurringErrorsInputSchema,
	ROIMetricsSchema,
	ROITrendSchema,
	SessionAnalyticsSchema,
	SessionAnalyticsSummaryComparisonSchema,
	SessionAnalyticsSummarySchema,
	SessionDetailInputSchema,
	SessionDetailSchema,
	SessionListInputSchema,
	SuccessRateSchema,
	TeamSummaryComparisonSchema,
	UsageTrendDataSchema,
	UserMappingSchema,
} from "./schemas/analytics.js";

export * from "./schemas/analytics.js";

export const HealthSchema = z.object({
	status: z.literal("ok"),
	timestamp: z.number(),
});

export const UserSchema = z.object({
	id: z.string(),
	email: z.string(),
	name: z.string(),
	image: z.string().nullable(),
});

export const SessionTagSchema = z.enum([
	"research",
	"new_feature",
	"bug_fix",
	"refactoring",

content.text:
All implementation is complete. Here's a summary of everything that was done:

## Summary of Changes

### Phase 1: Database Schema + Migration
- **`packages/sql-schema/src/auth-schema.ts`** — Added `organization`, `member`, `invitation` tables + `activeOrganizationId` column on `session` (already done by you)
- **`packages/sql-schema/db/migrations/0001_flippant_sharon_ventura.sql`** — Appended data backfill SQL that creates personal orgs (`id = user.id`) for existing users, adds them as owners, and sets `activeOrganizationId` on existing sessions

### Phase 2: API
- **`apps/api/src/auth.ts`** — Organization plugin + auto-create personal org hook (already done by you)
- **`apps/api/src/middleware.ts`** — Added `orgMiddleware` that extracts `activeOrganizationId` from session, falls back to `user.id`
- **`apps/api/src/router.ts`** — Added `listMyOrganizations` route, updated `me` to return `activeOrganizationId`, updated `ingestSession` to accept optional `organizationId` with membership verification
- **`packages/api-routes/src/index.ts`** — Added `OrganizationSchema`, `listMyOrganizations` contract, `organizationId` to ingest input, `activeOrganizationId` to `UserSchema`
- **8 handler files** — All 38 occurrences of `context.user.id` changed to `context.organizationId` via `orgMiddleware`

### Phase 3: Web App
- **`apps/web/src/lib/auth-client.ts`** — Added `organizationClient()` plugin
- **`apps/web/src/contexts/OrganizationContext.tsx`** — New context for org state, auto-sets active org, invalidates queries on switch
- **`apps/web/src/components/analytics/Sidebar.tsx`** — Replaced static header with org switcher dropdown (shows all orgs, manage/create links)
- **`apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/OrganizationPage.tsx`** — Members list, invite form with shareable link, pending invitations management
- **`apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/CreateOrgPage.tsx`** — Create new org form
- **`apps/web/src/pages/AcceptInvitationPage.tsx`** — Accept/decline invitation flow

### Phase 4: CLI
- **`apps/cli/src/lib/project-config.ts`** — Global `~/.rudel/projects.json` keyed by git remote URL or path
- **`apps/cli/src/lib/api-client.ts`** — Reusable oRPC client factory
- **`apps/cli/src/commands/enable.ts`** — Now fetches orgs and prompts for selection, saves to `~/.rudel/projects.json`
- **`apps/cli/src/commands/hooks/claude/session-end.ts`** — Reads org from project config, sends `organizationId` with upload
- **`apps/cli/src/commands/upload.ts`** — Reads org from project config, added `--org` flag override
- **`apps/cli/src/commands/set-org.ts`** — New command to change org for current repo
